name: Зимний Скиталец
description: Winter Walkers hone their craft in the bleak and frozen wilds of places like Icewind Dale. These ruthless, rimed Rangers hunt monsters that haunt arctic wastelands, eventually becoming frigid terrors themselves. Winter Walkers are well versed in the phenomena of Icewind Dale, including the latent magic of fallen Netherese cities, endemic monsters like yetis and crag cats, and the rising threat of Underdark invaders. Due to their cold pragmatism, terrifying magic, and mastery of the region, Winter Walkers are regarded with equal parts respect and fear.
shortDescription: Withstand the Horrors of Frigid Wastelands

details.bitingCold: {@b Biting Cold.} Damage from your weapon attacks, Ranger spells, and Ranger features ignores {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Cold damage.
details.frostResistance: {@b Frost Resistance.} You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Cold damage.
details.polarStrikes: {@b Polar Strikes.} When you hit a creature with an attack roll using a weapon, you can deal an extra {@damage 1d4} Cold damage to the target, which can take this extra damage only once per turn. When you reach Ranger level 11, this extra damage increases to {@damage 1d6}.

features.name: Hunter's Rime
features.description: Ice rimes you and your prey, protecting you and slowing them. When you cast {@spell Hunter's Mark|XPHB}, you gain {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to {@damage 1d10} plus your Ranger level. Additionally, while a creature is marked by your {@spell Hunter's Mark|XPHB}, it can't take the {@action Disengage|XPHB} action.

features.name: Winter Walker Spells
features.description: When you reach a Ranger level specified in the Winter Walker Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

features.name: Fortifying Soul
features.description: Your experience surviving harrowing environments allows you to bolster your allies in addition to yourself. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, choose a number of creatures you can see equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of one). Each chosen creature regains {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to {@damage 1d10} plus your Ranger level and has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws to avoid or end the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition for 1 hour. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

features.name: Chilling Retribution
features.description: When a creature hits you with an attack roll, you can take a {@action Reaction|XPHB} to force the creature to make a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, the target has the {@condition Stunned|XPHB} condition until the end of your next turn. While the target is {@condition Stunned|XPHB}, its Speed is reduced to 0 feet. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

features.name: Frozen Haunt
features.description: When you cast {@spell Hunter's Mark|XPHB}, you can adopt a ghostly, snowy form. This form lasts until the spell ends, and while you are in this form, you gain the following benefits. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} unless you expend a level 4+ spell slot (no action required).

details.frozenSoul: {@b Frozen Soul.} You have Immunity to Cold damage. When you first adopt this form and at the start of each of your subsequent turns, each creature of your choice in a 15-foot {@variantrule Emanation|XPHB} originating from you takes {@damage 2d4} Cold damage.
details.partiallyIncorporeal: {@b Partially Incorporeal.} You have Immunity to the {@condition Grappled|XPHB}, {@condition Prone|XPHB}, and {@condition Restrained|XPHB} conditions. You can move through creatures and objects as if they were {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB}, but you take {@damage 1d10} Force damage if you end your turn inside a creature or an object. If the form ends while you are inside a creature or an object, you are shunted to the nearest unoccupied space.
